data.cheatsheetContent:
You are a mathematician in the field of abstract algebra.

Decide whether source law A ({{ equation1 }}) implies law B ({{ equation2 }}) over all magmas (binary op *). Follow this protocol exactly.

## IMPORTANT
Never override a rule with algebra.
If a source-collapse lemma, contradiction motif, separator, or FALSE rule fires, stop immediately.
Only use extra algebra in STEP 0.
If no rule proves TRUE, answer FALSE.
check your work 

## STEP 0A: SOURCE-COLLAPSE LEMMAS
First check if A is bare (one side is a single variable).

### BARE NORMALIZATION:
Put the single variable on LEFT, call it x
Rename RHS variables by first appearance: y, z, w, ...
Keep full bracketing
Match exactly:
x = x*(y*(z*(x*y))) → LEFT PROJECTION (p*q = p)
x = x*((y*z)*(z*z)) → LEFT PROJECTION
x = (((y*z)*x)*z)*x → RIGHT PROJECTION (p*q = q)
If matched:
Collapse B (leftmost or rightmost variable)
Same → TRUE, else FALSE

### NON-BARE CONSTANT-PRODUCT:
Normalize A (rename only)
If LHS = x*y and RHS = U*V
and all x occur only in V
Then:
Non-variable terms → c, variables unchanged
Evaluate B → same = TRUE, else FALSE

### STEP 0B: BARE-SOURCE CONTRADICTION MOTIFS
Apply only if A is bare and STEP 0 did not fire.
Do NOT rename (only ensure x is on LEFT).
Compute on RHS:
rhsVars = distinct variables
rhsTotals = sorted counts (e.g. 2,2,1 → 122)
Lx, Rx = is leftmost/rightmost variable x
xTop = left / right / both (position at top split)
square = contains u*u
topShape = v-m, m-v, m-m
TRUE if any:
C1: rhsVars=4, ¬Lx, ¬Rx
C2: counts=311, ¬Lx, ¬Rx
C3: ¬Lx, xTop=left, square, m-v
C4: counts=211, ¬Lx, ¬Rx, xTop=right, v-m
C5: counts=2111, ¬Lx, ¬Rx, xTop=right, v-m
C6: rhsVars=3, xTop=right, v-m, x occurs twice
C7: rhsVars=3, ¬Lx, ¬Rx, xTop=left, m-v, x occurs twice
C8: rhsVars=3, Lx, xTop=left, m-v
C9: counts=221, Lx, ¬Rx, xTop=both, v-m
C10: counts=221, ¬Lx, Rx, x occurs twice
C11: counts=311, ¬Lx, ¬Rx, xTop=right, v-m
C12: counts=311, ¬Lx, ¬Rx, xTop=left, m-v
C13: counts=2111, ¬Lx, Rx
C14: B bare AND counts=311 AND Rx AND ¬square

## STEP 1: COMPUTE FEATURES
For each equation E:
size = total variable occurrences
vars = distinct variables
imb = sum of |left-right| counts
bare = one side is single variable
LP = leftmost vars match
RP = rightmost vars match
SET = same variable set both sides
XOR = same parity per variable
AB = exact counts match

## STEP 2: SEPARATORS (→ FALSE)
If any:
LP(A) and not LP(B)
RP(A) and not RP(B)
SET(A) and not SET(B)
XOR(A) and not XOR(B)
AB(A) and not AB(B)

## STEP 3: FALSE GUARDS
G1:
bare(A), not bare(B)
size equal
vars(B) < vars(A)
imb(B) > imb(A)
XOR(A)
G2:
bare(A), vars(A)=2
not bare(B), vars(B) > vars(A)
size(B) ≥ size(A)
G3:
not bare(A), vars(A) ≥ 5
not bare(B), vars(B) < vars(A)
size equal
G4:
not bare(A), not bare(B)
size equal
vars(B) ≤ vars(A)-2
Any → FALSE

## STEP 4: STRUCTURAL RULES
Let s, v, i for size/vars/imb.
T4: bare(A), vA≥4, vB=2 → TRUE
T6: bare(A), not RP(A), XOR(A), vB=2, RP(B) → TRUE
T7: not bare(A), vA=3, iA=2, vB=3, not XOR(B) → TRUE
T8: vA=3, not XOR(A), bare(B), not RP(B) → TRUE
F1: vB = vA → FALSE
F2: iB < iA → FALSE
F3: sB < sA → FALSE
T1: bare(A), vA≥3, sB > sA → TRUE
T2: bare(A), bare(B), vA≥3, vB>vA, iB>iA → TRUE
T5: vB < vA, iB > iA → TRUE
STEP 5: EXTENDED
D3:
All variables in A have equal TOTAL counts
vB < vA
B is x = x*(...)
→ TRUE
Else → FALSE
REASONING STYLE
State:
computed features
whether STEP 0 / 0B fired
any separator/guard
final rule used
  OUTPUT FORMAT:
VERDICT: TRUE or FALSE
REASONING: non-empty explanation
PROOF: required if TRUE
COUNTEREXAMPLE: required if FALSE
